# Customer Fixture Workflow Guide

## Customer-project portability

Public skills operate in customer-project mode by default.

Use project-local artifacts when present and relevant.

Do not require RigorLoop repository-internal specs, docs, root governance, reports, or follow-up files in this customer fixture.

When local guidance is absent, use portable defaults where safe and block on ambiguity where no safe default exists.

## Artifact locations

| Artifact | Location |
|---|---|
| specs | `specs/` |
| plans | `docs/plans/` |
| change root | `docs/changes/<change-id>/` |

# Customer Feature

## Status

approved

## Goal and context

The customer fixture exposes a small local source/test surface for portability benchmark scenarios.

## Requirements

R1. The fixture MUST have local source and test files.

R2. The fixture MUST NOT require RigorLoop repository-internal governance files.

## Acceptance criteria

- Local workflow guidance is present.
- Root `AGENTS.md` and `CONSTITUTION.md` are absent.

item.text: The handoff identifies only a generic fixture milestone; it does not name the greeting suffix option. I’m checking the local source and tests named by the change metadata to see whether a safe first implementation action can be derived without expanding into unavailable internal artifacts.

- evidence used: `docs/plans/customer-plan.md`, `docs/workflows.md`, `docs/changes/example-change/change.yaml`, `specs/customer-feature.md`, `src/app.js`, `tests/app.test.js`
- whether local `docs/workflows.md` was used: yes
- portable default or ambiguity behavior: customer-project portable default used; local artifacts were sufficient to show the handoff, but ambiguous for greeting suffix scope, so block rather than infer requirements
- attempted reliance on absent RigorLoop internals: no
- first implementation action: obtain or update approved local scope for the greeting suffix option before editing; after approval, the first code action would be adding a failing suffix-option test in `tests/app.test.js`
- blocker: local plan/spec handoff does not mention or approve a greeting suffix option behavior
